bibliographic_data.title: Nexusformer: Nonlinear Attention Expansion for Stable and Inheritable Transformer Scaling
bibliographic_data.abstract: Scaling Transformers typically necessitates training larger models from scratch, as standard architectures struggle to expand without discarding learned representations. We identify the primary bottleneck in the attention mechanism’s linear projections, which strictly confine feature extraction to fixed-dimensional subspaces, limiting both expressivity and incremental capacity. To address this, we introduce Nexusformer, which replaces linear Q/K/V projections with a Nexus-Rank layer, a three-stage nonlinear mapping driven by dual activations in progressively higher dimensional spaces. This design overcomes the linearity constraint and enables lossless structured growth: new capacity can be injected along two axes via zero-initialized blocks that preserve pretrained knowledge.

core_content.novelty_claim: Nexusformer introduces a Nexus-Rank layer to replace linear Q/K/V projections in Transformers, enabling nonlinear mappings and lossless structured growth.
core_content.key_methodology: Nexusformer uses a three-stage nonlinear mapping driven by dual activations in progressively higher dimensional spaces via the Nexus-Rank layer. This design allows for enhanced feature interaction while maintaining compatibility with standard components.
outcomes: Nexusformer matches Tokenformer’s perplexity using up to 41.5% less training compute during progressive scaling (240M to 440M).
Zero initialization induces a stable convergence trajectory, allowing for geometric scaling law predictions across expansion scales.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Nexusformer addresses the limitations of standard Transformers in terms of scalability by introducing Nexus-Rank layers. These nonlinear mappings enable enhanced feature interaction and lossless growth, making it possible to expand models without discarding learned representations. The experiments show that Nexusformer can achieve competitive or superior results on reasoning benchmarks with reduced training compute compared to Tokenformer.
